projects.title: I Am Not Your Negro
projects.synopsis: In 1979, James Baldwin wrote a letter to his literary agent describing his next project, "Remember This House." The book was to be a revolutionary, personal account of the lives and assassinations of three of his close friends: Medgar Evers, Malcolm X and Martin Luther King, Jr. At the time of Baldwin's death in 1987, he left behind only 30 completed pages of this manuscript. Filmmaker Raoul Peck envisions the book James Baldwin never finished.

projects.title: Uncut Gems
projects.synopsis: A fast-talking New York jeweler with a bottomless gambling habit bets everything on a rare Ethiopian opal, juggling debts, family and collectors in a relentless two-hour panic attack. Anxiety rendered as pure cinema.

projects.title: Fitzcarraldo
projects.synopsis: An obsessive dreamer hauls a 320-ton steamship over a mountain in the Peruvian Amazon to fund an opera house in the jungle. A monument to mad ambition, filmed for real.

projects.title: Akira
projects.synopsis: In a neon Neo-Tokyo, a biker gang member develops catastrophic psychic powers that threaten to level the city again. The landmark that redefined what animation could do.

projects.title: Ping Pong the Animation
projects.synopsis: Two childhood friends chase very different dreams through the world of competitive table tennis. Kinetic, elastic and unexpectedly tender — a sports story about what a hero is.

projects.title: The Holy Mountain
projects.synopsis: A thief is initiated by an alchemist into a group of powerful figures seeking the secret of immortality atop a sacred mountain. A blasphemous, dazzling fever dream.

projects.title: Doomed Megalopolis
projects.synopsis: A vengeful sorcerer conspires to raze imperial Tokyo by awakening the vengeful spirit that sleeps beneath it. Occult horror sprawling across decades of the city’s history.

projects.title: Antichrist
projects.synopsis: After the loss of their child, a grieving couple retreat to a cabin called Eden, where nature and grief turn monstrous. Beautiful, brutal and impossible to shake.

projects.title: Perfect Blue
projects.synopsis: A pop idol quits her group to become an actress and loses her grip on where her image ends and she begins. A psychological thriller that blurs reality and performance.

projects.title: Stalker
projects.synopsis: A guide leads a writer and a scientist through a forbidden Zone to a room that grants one’s deepest desire. A slow, hypnotic pilgrimage through faith and doubt.

projects.title: Come and See
projects.synopsis: A boy joins the partisan resistance in occupied Belarus and witnesses atrocity after atrocity until his face itself becomes a record of the war. Perhaps the most harrowing war film ever made.

projects.title: Tampopo
projects.synopsis: A trucker helps a widow perfect her ramen shop in this playful "ramen western," braided with vignettes about the sensual, absurd relationship between people and food.

projects.title: Paprika
projects.synopsis: When a device that lets therapists enter patients’ dreams is stolen, the boundary between dream and reality collapses. Satoshi Kon’s final feature is a riot of color and invention.

projects.title: Suspiria
projects.synopsis: An American dancer arrives at a German ballet academy that hides a witches’ coven. A delirium of color, sound and dread — horror as pure sensory overload.

projects.title: Belladonna of Sadness
projects.synopsis: A wronged peasant woman makes a pact with the devil in this hand-painted, watercolor erotic epic. A hypnotic, transgressive landmark of adult animation.

projects.title: Aguirre, the Wrath of God
projects.synopsis: A conquistador leads a doomed expedition down the Amazon in search of El Dorado, his ambition curdling into madness. Herzog and Kinski’s first and most feverish collaboration.
